dataset.title: Dataset: Final results of the implementation of the Carbon Measurement Protocol in Mangrove Ecosystems in the Districts of Remedios, San Félix and San Lorenzo
dataset.abstract: This dataset contains the findings of a carbon stock inventory conducted in 2016 in support of a project dedicated to the protection of carbon reserves and sinks in the mangroves and protected areas of Panama, which is part of the International Initiative for Climate Protection. The aim of this project was to measure and quantify carbon stocks at the ecosystem level in the mangroves of the Remedios San Félix and San Lorenzo districts of the Chiriquí Province. Fieldwork was carried out across 22 different sites, and 123 plots, where vegetation was surveyed, and soils were sampled to provide estimates of total ecosystem carbon stock.
dataset.intellectualRights: This dataset is listed under a Creative Commons BY 4.0 and can be used with attribution. https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/

attribute.attributeDefinition: Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Code indicating what type of device was used to collect soil depth profiles.

codeDefinition.definition: A half cylinder coring device in which the coring section is open, not sealed off by a fin

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code indicating whether live roots were included or excluded from carbon assessments.

codeDefinition.definition: Roots and rhizomes were included in dry bulk density and or organic matter and carbon measurements

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code indicating whether or not sediment was sieved prior to carbon measurements.

codeDefinition.definition: Sediment was not sieved prior to analysis for organics.

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code indicating how the authors qualified or quantified compaction of the core.

codeDefinition.definition: Compaction was not specified.

attribute.attributeDefinition: Temperature at which samples were dried to measure dry bulk density. This can include either samples that were freeze dried or oven dried.
Any notable codes regarding how the authors quantified dry bulk density.

codeDefinition.definition: No additional details regarding bulk density methodology were provided
Bulk density methodology did not specify drying temperature or length, only that samples were dried to a constant mass

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code indicating whether fraction carbon was measured or estimated as a function of organic matter.

codeDefinition.definition: Fraction carbon was measured as opposed to modeled

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code indicating the method for which fraction carbon was measured or modeled (Note: regression based models are permitted, but the use of the Bemmelen factor [0.58 gOC gOM-1] is discouraged).

codeDefinition.definition: Each sample presented was measured using Elemental Analysis
No additional details were provided regarding fraction carbon methodologies

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code indicating whether fraction_carbon refers to organic or total carbon.

codeDefinition.definition: Author specified that fraction carbon measurements were of total carbon.

attribute.attributeDefinition: Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Site identification code unique to each study.
Positional latitude in decimal degree WGS84.
Positional longitude in decimal degree WGS84.
Code indicating how latitude and longitude were determined.

codeDefinition.definition: Any other technique resulting in positional error > 30 meters.

attribute.attributeDefinition: Any relevant submitter generated notes on how latitude and longitude were determined.
Carbon calculated from the debris pieces measured.
Carbon calculated from the understory cover.
Belowground root carbon, summarized across trees and saplings.
Name of location where one or more sites were sampled.
Carbon calculated for all live trees across all the plots within a given site.
Carbon calculated for all dead trees across all the plots within a given site.
Belowground carbon across all plots within a given site.
Aboveground carbon across all plots within a given site.
Total ecosystem carbon, calculated by summarizing the above and belowground carbon.
Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Site identification code unique to each study.
Core identification code unique to each site.
Minimum depth of a soil horizon of which the sample is representative.
Maximum depth of a soil horizon of which the sample is representative.
Dry mass per unit volume of a soil sample. This does not include ash free bulk density.
Mass of carbon relative to sample dry mass.
Any other submitter generated notes specific to the depth interval.
Soil carbon calculated as the product of the fraction carbon content, dry bulk density, and depth interval.
Water content for a given interval, calculated as wet weight - dry weight/wet weight.
Dry weight of the sediment sample, which was used to determine bulk density.
Calculated mass of carbon per unit volume of a soil sample.
Color of the soil noted for a given depth interval.
The identifier of the plot which occurs along the sampling transect within a site.
Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Site identification code unique to each study.
Year of observation or sample collection.
Numeric month of year of core collection.
Numeric day of month of core collection.
The identifier of the plot which occurs along the sampling transect within a site.
Basal area calculated for the plant, based on it's diameter.
Basal area density, calculated with respect to the plot area.
Radius of the circular plot. A 7-meter radius plot was used to measure individuals with a DHB > 5cm, and a 2-meter radius plot was used to measure individuals with DBH < 5cm.
Species of the plant.
Diameter of the trunk or stem.
Height at which the diameter of the trunk or stem was measured.
Code indicating how the diameter of the trunk or stem was measured.

codeDefinition.definition: Diameter at chest height, measured at 1.3 meters.
Diameter measured above the last prop root, usually applying to a Rhizophora species.

attribute.attributeDefinition: Code indicating whether the plant measured was alive or dead.

codeDefinition.definition: Plant measured was alive.
Plant measured was dead.

attribute.attributeDefinition: The decay status assigned to dead trees which are still standing (source Kauffman and Donato 2012).

codeDefinition.definition: Small branches and twigs are retained, but leaves are absent.
No twigs or small branches, and may have lost a portion of large branches.
Few or no branches, standing stem only, or may be broken-topped.

attribute.attributeDefinition: Height where the canopy of the tree begins.
Height measurement or estimate.
Indication of canopy condition.
Aboveground plant organic matter, calculated using a general equation for the Americas (Howard et al. 2014)
Aboveground carbon calculated from the biomass derived using a general equation for the Americas by applying a carbon conversion factor of 0.49.
Aboveground plant organic matter, calculated using species specific equations.
Aboveground carbon calculated from the biomass derived using species specific equations by applying a carbon conversion factor of 0.49.
Mass of carbon calculated for plants belowground.
Mass of plant organic matter belowground.
Any relevant notes about the plant specimen or how it was measured.
Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Site identification code unique to each study.
The identifier of the plot which occurs along the sampling transect within a site.
The length of transect along which downed pieces of debris were measured.
Diameter of the debris piece where it crosses the transect.
The decay status assigned to fallen pieces of wood debris.

codeDefinition.definition: Debris piece is hard, indicating higher wood density.
Debris piece is deteriorated but not yet rotten, indicating intermediate wood density.
Debris piece is deteriorated, indicating lower wood density.

attribute.attributeDefinition: Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Site identification code unique to each study.
The identifier of the plot which occurs along the sampling transect within a site.
Carbon calculated from the understory cover.
Factor used to convert organic matter to organic carbon, based on the percent carbon for a given tree species or biomass component.
Percent of understory in the subplot.
Biomass calculated for each subplot.
Area of subplot.
Carbon calculated for each subplot by applying a carbon conversion factor of 0.47 to the understory biomass.
Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Site identification code unique to each study.
The identifier of the plot which occurs along the sampling transect within a site.
Species of the plant.
Area of subplot.
Number of seedlings counted in a given subplot area.
Range of heights measured for seedlings in a given subplot area.
Unique identifier for the study made up of the first author's family name, as well as the second author's family name or et al. if more than three, then publication year separated by underscores.
Site identification code unique to each study.
The identifier of the plot which occurs along the sampling transect within a site.
Salinity measured.
pH measured.
Temperature of the soil measured.
Observed presence or absence of organic material in soil.
Observed presence or absence of inorganic mineral in soil.
Observed presence or absence of sand in soil.
Observed presence or absence of clay in soil.
Observed presence or absence of silt in soil.
Any relevant notes about how environmental parameters were measured.

unit.name: nominalMonth
unit.description: one month excluding leap seconds and leap days, 2628000 seconds
